chunk_summary.idea_trajectory_justification: The vast majority of the chunk is dedicated to structured participant introductions and reading the facilitator's prompts, with only the final minute introducing a substantive research topic.
chunk_summary.collective_engagement_justification: Non-speaking participants consistently maintain eye contact with the screen, nod during introductions, and share smiles and laughter in response to a joke about being hungry.
chunk_summary.cross_disciplinary_bridging_description: Merging molecular scale modeling with ecological scales.
chunk_summary.problem_specificity_justification: Silvie narrows the domain to vector-borne diseases and climate-dependent modeling, but a specific research question has not yet been articulated.
chunk_summary.decision_crystallization_justification: The group is just beginning to share individual interests and has not yet identified a shared direction.
chunk_summary.complementarity_recognition_quote: I am not a modeler at all... but I like to collaborate with mathematical modelers.
chunk_summary.skill_gap_description: Silvie explicitly notes her inability to write differential equations and her reliance on mathematical modelers.
chunk_summary.notable_observation: The group spent the first nine minutes entirely on structured introductions and reading prompts in silence, leaving very little time for actual ideation in this segment.

ideas_currently_on_table: Modeling vector-borne disease patterns and climate dependence
chunk_summaries: The group began the session with Wilfred van der Donk outlining the process and initiating a round of introductions. Each participant shared their disciplinary background and current research focus, ranging from RNA virology and biophysical modeling to parasite-host interactions and horizontal gene transfer. The tone was polite and structured, and the chunk ended with Kristin Koutmou volunteering to be the reporter before the group transitioned to discussing the prompts.

The group spent the majority of the chunk conducting structured introductions, with each participant sharing their background and expertise. Facilitator Rebecca Garabed then shared her screen to display the discussion prompts, giving the group time to read them silently. In the final minute, Silvie Huijben initiated the substantive discussion by proposing a focus on modeling vector-borne diseases and their dependence on climate.
